DecideRequest.description:
Operator's decision on one proposal.

``rationale`` is optional but encouraged — Phase 7-E's audit trail
is the same ledger that Phase 7-A laid down; an unexplained
rejection is a near-future regret. The UI should default-prompt.
DecideRequest.title: DecideRequest

ProposalView.description:
One proposal as it appears on the wire.

Single-user-mode drops ``owner_id`` + ``workspace_id`` — they're
sentinel UUIDs on every row and add noise. Phase 9+ multi-tenant
work re-surfaces them. ``decided`` is a convenience flag so
dashboards can render decision status without joining client-side.
ProposalView.title: ProposalView

RunSummaryResponse.description: One row from the pipeline_runs ledger as the dashboard sees it.
RunSummaryResponse.title: RunSummaryResponse

TriggerBody.description:
v2 trigger request — extensible by ``type`` (correlation_analysis,
recovery_check).
TriggerBody.title: TriggerBody

post.description:
Receive a metric batch from HealthSave iOS app.

Expected payload:
{
    "metric": "heart_rate",
    "batch_index": 0,
    "total_batches": 1,
    "samples": [
        {"date": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z", "qty": 72, "source": "Apple Watch"},
        ...
    ]
}

get.description: Return record counts so the iOS app knows what's synced.

Return recent anomaly findings from the analysis engine.

Reads ``analysis_findings`` where ``finding_type='anomaly'``, ordered
by ``created_at DESC``. Optional ``since`` limits rows to those
created at-or-after the timestamp. Optional ``severity`` is a
comma-separated list (``info,watch,alert``) matched against the
finding's severity column. SQL lives in
``storage.timescale.briefings`` — this handler does parameter
validation and wire-shape mapping only.
Return today's daily briefing narrative.
Return the most recent daily briefing + weekly summary narratives.

Recent rows from the pipeline_runs ledger.

The ledger is written by the ``apps/worker`` APScheduler listener
(Phase 4B). This route is the read-side surface — newest first,
optional ``job_kind`` filter, capped at 200 per request.

Status values: pending, running, succeeded, failed, cancelled,
skipped. ``triggered_by`` is one of: scheduler, manual, api, event.

parameters.description: Filter to a specific scheduler job (e.g. 'daily_briefing').
parameters.name: job_kind

schema.description: Filter to a specific scheduler job (e.g. 'daily_briefing').
schema.title: Job Kind

get.description:
Return recent trend findings from the analysis engine.

SQL lives in ``storage.timescale.briefings`` — this handler does
parameter validation (period format) and wire-shape mapping only.

parameters.description: Optional day period filter such as 30d or 90d
parameters.name: period

schema.description: Optional day period filter such as 30d or 90d
schema.title: Period

post.description:
Run an ad-hoc analysis job inline.

``daily_briefing`` and ``trend_analysis`` run synchronously against
``app.state.analysis_engine``. Future long-running job types
(weekly, correlation, etc.) should dispatch to the ``apps/worker``
service via a Postgres NOTIFY queue rather than running inline —
the API process no longer carries a scheduler in v2.

get.description: Return the current week's summary narrative.

List recent proposals.

Reads through the agent repository. Per the Phase 5 storage-zone rule, the
route never composes its own query against ``action_proposals``.

parameters.description: When true, only return proposals without a matching action_decisions row. The dashboard's review queue uses this; full audit views set false.
parameters.name: undecided_only

schema.description: When true, only return proposals without a matching action_decisions row. The dashboard's review queue uses this; full audit views set false.
schema.title: Undecided Only

post.description:
Record the operator's decision on one proposal.

Writes a single row into ``action_decisions`` via
:class:`storage.ports.AgentRepository`. The decision is append-only —
re-deciding (e.g. flipping a rejection to an
approval) writes a new row, and downstream readers take the
newest. The supervisor / executor path that picks up an approved
proposal is Phase 7-F territory; Phase 7-E only persists the
decision.

Errors:

  * ``404`` — no proposal with that id. We intentionally don't
    leak whether the id is malformed-but-syntactically-valid vs.
    truly absent; both surface as 404.

get.description: Latest-activity fingerprint with ETag/304 semantics.
List experiments, newest first; optional ``?status=`` filter.

post.description:
Create an experiment from a testable candidate.

Validates the pair with the pure readiness classifier: the pair must be
``testable`` *and* the supplied ``lever_metric_id`` must actually be the
controllable lever (not the outcome). On success the experiment is created
and an immediate retrospective (observational) preview is computed over
existing history — best-effort, so a thin-history install still creates the
experiment cleanly.

get.description:
Ranked correlation candidates annotated with experiment readiness.

Strongest correlations first — the top of the list is the best candidate to
promote into an experiment. Deduped to one row per metric pair (the strongest
seen), since the same pair recurs across correlation runs.

schema.title: Response List Candidates Api V2 Experiments Candidates Get

get.description: One experiment: definition + phase calendar + progress + latest results.

post.description:
(Re)compute the controlled ABAB result now and return the refreshed view.

Completes the experiment automatically once its window has fully elapsed.

schema.title: Response List Export Metrics Api V2 Export Metrics Get

get.description: Persisted cross-metric correlation findings, newest first.

parameters.description: Optional day window such as 30d or 90d
parameters.name: period

schema.description: Optional day window such as 30d or 90d
schema.title: Period
Response List Correlations Api V2 Insights Correlations Get

get.description:
Recent structured findings (the evidence feed), newest first.

Each row carries its ``structured_data`` so the evidence card can show the
calculation behind a finding (effect size, window, p-value, …). Optional
``?type=`` narrows to one kind.

parameters.description: Optional finding kind (anomaly / trend / correlation / summary).
parameters.name: type

schema.description: Optional finding kind (anomaly / trend / correlation / summary).
schema.title: Type
Response List Findings Api V2 Insights Findings Get

get.description: Most recent daily-briefing + weekly-summary narratives (the weekly-brief card).

schema.title: Response Latest Narratives Api V2 Insights Latest Get

post.description:
Run an analysis job on demand.

Supports ``correlation_analysis`` and ``recovery_check``. Each checks its
config block is enabled, runs the Brain-1 engine job inline through the
pipeline_runs ledger, and reports completed vs skipped.

schema.title: Response Trigger Api V2 Insights Trigger Post

get.description: Current narrator posture (no secrets).

schema.title: Response Get Intelligence Api V2 Intelligence Get

put.description: Apply mode + primary + fallback. Does NOT grant cloud egress (see consent).

schema.title: Response Put Intelligence Api V2 Intelligence Put

post.description: Grant / revoke the cloud-egress opt-in (the separate consent step, D5).

schema.title: Response Post Consent Api V2 Intelligence Consent Post

get.description:
Probe the known local Ollama endpoints so the UI can auto-fill "Local".

Returns each candidate's reachability + installed models. No DB, no health
data, no egress (the targets are inside the trust boundary). Lets a
non-technical user click "Detect" instead of typing a base URL.

schema.title: Response Detect Local Api V2 Intelligence Detect Local Get

post.description: Probe a provider (SSRF-guarded, one token, no health data); audit it.

schema.title: Response Test Connection Api V2 Intelligence Test Connection Post

get.description: Return the v2 version axes. Unauthenticated, read-only, no health data.

schema.title: Response V2 Meta Api V2 Meta Get

get.description: The full canonical metric catalog (ontology-driven, no DB hit).

schema.title: Response List Metrics Api V2 Metrics Get

get.description:
One metric's time-series over a multi-timescale window.

``stream_id`` optionally narrows the series to a single device stream
(one physical emitter); omitted returns the fused series across all
streams, unchanged.

schema.title: Response Metric Series Api V2 Metrics  Metric Id  Series Get

get.description:
The host's egress posture: provider, local-vs-cloud, and what may leave.

``raw_observations_leave_host`` is always ``false`` — the privacy promise is
an invariant, enforced unconditionally by the policy regardless of opt-in.

schema.title: Response Privacy Api V2 Privacy Get

get.description: Per-metric coverage + analyzability, plus source attribution and freshness.

schema.title: Response Readiness Api V2 Readiness Get

get.description: Egress-relevant audit events (newest first) + ingest freshness.

schema.title: Response List Receipts Api V2 Receipts Get

get.description:
Many metrics' time-series in one request (the dashboard's grid fetch).

``ids`` is a comma-separated list of metric ids. Unknown ids come back as
per-item ``{"metric_id", "error"}`` entries instead of failing the whole
request, so one bad id can't blank a dashboard. Each known item carries
the exact shape of ``/metrics/{id}/series`` minus the redundant
range/start/end (hoisted to the envelope).

schema.title: Response Metric Series Batch Api V2 Series Get

get.description:
Return a no-secret setup fingerprint for humans and clients.

This is intentionally unauthenticated: it exposes no health data and helps
users distinguish the Data Hub API from nearby Grafana/Homepage ports before
they troubleshoot keys or sync.

schema.title: Response Setup Diagnostics Api V2 Setup Diagnostics Get
Response Whoop Webhook Api V2 Sources Whoop Webhook Post

get.description: Detect suspicious sync behavior visible from server receipts.

schema.title: Response Sync Anomalies Api V2 Sync Anomalies Get

get.description: Return metric-level receipt and destination sample coverage.

schema.title: Response Sync Coverage Api V2 Sync Coverage Get

get.description: Summarize the most recently observed HealthSave sync run.

schema.title: Response Latest Sync Run Api V2 Sync Runs Latest Get

get.description: Return the delivery receipt summary for one HealthSave sync run.

schema.title: Response Sync Run Api V2 Sync Runs  Sync Run Id  Get
